BotIncomingWebhookRequest.description: Request body for the incoming webhook endpoint. Accepts any number of custom key-value parameters that are passed directly to the bot's incoming_webhook_handler Deluge script. Up to 50 additional parameters are supported, each with a maximum value length of 10,000 characters. There are no fixed or required fields.

custom_parameters.description:
Pass additional key-value pairs in the request body to provide context to the bot's handler. These parameters are user-defined and can carry any metadata relevant to your integration.

Rules for Custom Parameters
  • Up to 50 custom parameters are supported per request.
  • Each parameter value can be up to 10,000 characters.
  • All values must be of type string.
  • Parameter keys should be unique to avoid overwriting values in the bot handler.
Common Use Cases
  • Webhook Verification: Some platforms send a challenge_key during webhook registration to confirm that the endpoint is reachable. Your bot handler should echo it back in the response to complete the handshake.
  • CI/CD & Monitoring: Pass parameters like alert_type, build_status, or environment to notify teams about pipeline failures or infrastructure incidents in real time.
  • CRM & E-commerce: Send deal updates, order status changes, and customer activity metadata to keep sales and support teams informed without manual intervention.

BotIncomingWebhookResponse.description: Response returned after a successful incoming webhook message delivery. Contains the list of user IDs that received the message.

user_ids.description: List of user IDs to whom the bot message was successfully delivered.

NoResponse.description: Represents an empty successful response where no payload is returned.

Response Code.description: HTTP response code indicating no content is returned.

BotGetResponse.description: Response returned when retrieving the details of a specific bot.

url.description: API endpoint URL for the retrieved bot resource.

data.description: Full configuration and details of the bot.

channel_participation.description: Channel interaction capabilities of the bot.

id.description: Unique ID of the handler (present for menu_handler).

name.description: Display name of the menu action (present for menu_handler).

icon.description: Icon reference for the menu action (present for menu_handler).

position.description: Display position of the menu action (present for menu_handler).

execution_type.description: Script execution engine used by the bot.

status.description: Current operational status of the bot.

type.description: Bot category. Always custom for user-created bots.

default.description: Indicates whether this is the default bot.

subscriber_count.description: Number of users subscribed to the bot.

calls.description: Indicates whether bot calls are enabled or disabled.

BotCreateResponse.description: Response returned after successfully creating a bot.

url.description: API endpoint URL for the created bot resource.

unique_name.description: System-generated unique identifier for the bot (derived from name, cannot be changed).

name.description: Display name of the bot as provided in the request.

id.description: Unique numeric ID assigned to the bot.

description.description: Description of the bot as provided in the request.
description.example: Your client care co-pilot streamlining support by providing instant customer context and easy access to critical resources.

scope.description: Access level of the bot as provided in the request.

team_ids.description: Team IDs mapped to the bot (present when scope is team).

status_messages.description: Status messages assigned to the bot.
example: Here to help you with client support!

channel_participation.description: Channel interaction capabilities of the bot.

calls.description: Indicates whether bot calls are enabled or disabled.

handlers.description: List of handlers configured for the bot. Empty at creation time.

creator.description: Details of the user who created the bot.

execution_type.description: Script execution engine used by the bot.

status.description: Current operational status of the bot.

type.description: Bot category. Always custom for user-created bots.

default.description: Indicates whether this is the default bot for the organization.

subscriber_count.description: Number of users subscribed to the bot.

GenericResponse.description: Generic successful response envelope used for bot APIs where detailed payload schema varies.

url.description: Endpoint URL associated with the response.

type.description: Resource type returned by the API.

url.description: API endpoint URL for the listed resources.

next_token.description: Pagination token to fetch the next page of results.

sync_token.description: Token used for incremental synchronization to fetch changes since the last sync.

deleted.description: IDs of deleted bots returned during incremental sync.

channel_participation.description: Channel interaction capabilities of the bot.

id.description: Unique ID of the handler (present for menu_handler).

name.description: Display name of the menu action (present for menu_handler).

icon.description: Icon reference for the menu action (present for menu_handler).

position.description: Display position of the menu action (present for menu_handler).

execution_type.description: Script execution engine used by the bot.

status.description: Current operational status of the bot.

type.description: Bot category. Always custom for user-created bots.

default.description: Indicates whether this is the default bot.

subscriber_count.description: Number of users subscribed to the bot.

calls.description: Indicates whether bot calls are enabled or disabled.

BotHandlerCreateResponse.description: Response schema returned when a new bot handler is created successfully.

url.description: Endpoint URL of the newly created handler resource.

data.description: Configuration details of the created handler.

script.description: Deluge source code of the handler.

return_type.description: Return type expected from the handler script.

params.description: List of runtime parameters passed to the handler script.

param_type.description: Data type of the parameter (e.g., MAP, STRING, BOOLEAN).

BotHandlerDetailsResponse.description: Response schema returned when fetching the full details of a specific bot handler.

url.description: Endpoint URL of the handler resource.

data.description: Full configuration and metadata of the handler.

script.description: Deluge source code of the handler.

name.description: Display name of the handler (used for menu-type handlers).

icon.description: Icon reference for the handler, applicable for menu handlers.

return_type.description: Return type expected from the handler script.

params.description: List of parameters passed to the handler script at runtime.

param_type.description: Data type of the parameter (e.g., MAP, STRING).

data.name: IT Help Desk

BotHandlerResponse.description: Response schema for bot handler create, update, and fetch operations.

url.description: Endpoint URL for the handler resource.

BotHandlerLogsResponse.description: Response schema for bot handler execution logs.

url.description: Endpoint URL for the execution log resource.

debugInfo.description: Debug information entries as serialized JSON strings.

items.description: Serialized JSON string containing debug trace information with timestamp and message details.

message.example: The request cannot be performed. Usually because of malformed parameter or missing parameter.

BotSubscribersResponse.description: Represents the response structure for fetching subscribers of a specific bot. Contains metadata and a list of subscriber details.

url.description: The API endpoint URL to retrieve the list of subscribers for the specified bot.

type.description: The type of the response object. Typically indicates the resource or entity being described.

sync_token.description: A token used to synchronize and track the state of the subscribers list across requests.

data.description: An array containing subscriber details associated with the bot.

BotSubscriber.description: Detailed information about an individual subscriber of the bot.

email_id.description: The email address of the subscriber, used for identification and communication.

user_id.description: A unique identifier assigned to the subscriber within the system.

name.description: The full name of the subscriber.

Trigger-Bot-Call-Request.description: Represents the request body for triggering a bot call with a message, recipients, retries, loops, and optional actions.

text.description:
The message to be dictated during the call.
Limit: 500 characters.

user_ids.description:
List of user ZUIDs or emails.
Limit: 10 entries.

items.description: User identifier, which can be either a ZUID or an email address.

retry.description:
Number of retries if the user misses the call. System will retry after 2 minutes.
Maximum Attempts: 3

loop.description:
Number of times the message will be dictated.
Maximum: 3

actions.description:
List of actions to be displayed as buttons during the call.
Each action includes a label, icon, and a specific action with its associated data.
Limit: 5

items.description: Defines the structure of an action button shown during the bot call.

label.description:
Text displayed on the button.
Maximum Length: 20 characters.

icon.description: Icon identifier for the button. Can be an image URL or a supported icon keyword (e.g., tick, preview, url). View supported icons

hint.description:
Tooltip or additional message (optional).
Maximum Length: 100 characters.

key.description: Optional key reference. This can be used to identify the button in the bot handler when the action is triggered. Maximum Length: 100 characters.

action.description: An object containing the type of action the button should perform.

type.description:
The action type for a button.
Refer to Message Card Buttons for allowed action types and their corresponding data requirements.

data.description:
An object with properties for each action type. The required properties depend on the action type specified.
Refer to Message Card Buttons for details on the expected data structure for each action type.

example.text: We've detected an issue with Zylker Servers. Our team is working to resolve it at the earliest.

Bot-call-response.description: Represents the response returned after triggering a bot call, including the alert instance ID and associated users.

user_ids.description: List of user ZUIDs associated with the alert instance

items.description: User identifier represented as a ZUID.

id.description: Unique ID of the alert instance

BotCreate.description: Payload for creating a new bot.

execution_type.description:
Defines how the created bot will execute its handlers. The execution type determines the scripting environment for the bot's logic.
Allowed values:
  • deluge: Bot handlers will be implemented using Deluge scripts, allowing for complex logic and integration with Zoho services.
  • webhook: Bot handlers will trigger external webhooks, enabling integration with third-party services and custom backend logic.
Default value: deluge

execution_url.description:
Required if execution_type is set to webhook.
  • The URL that will be called when the bot's handlers are triggered.
  • This should be a publicly accessible endpoint that can receive POST requests from the Cliq platform.

name.description:
Display name of the bot. Must be unique across the platform and can be updated later.
Note: The unique_name (used to identify the bot in API calls) is auto-assigned by Cliq on creation and cannot be changed, even if the display name is updated.
Maximum Length: 20 characters.

description.description:
Description of the bot. Provides details about the bot's functionality and purpose.
Maximum Length: 300 characters.
description.example: Your client care co-pilot streamlining support by providing instant customer context and easy access to critical resources.

scope.description:
Access level for the bot. Determines the visibility and availability of the bot within the organization.
Allowed values:
  • organization: Bot is available to all users in the organization.
  • team: Bot is available only to specified teams within the organization.
  • personal: Bot is available only to the creator but can be shared with other users.

team_ids.description:
List of team_ids that are allowed to access the bot when scope is set to team.
Maximum limit: 4 team IDs can be provided, and this is mandatory when the bot's scope is set to team.

status_messages.description:
Status messages shown for the bot. These messages will be displayed in the bot's profile or when users interact with the bot.
Maximum limit: 10 messages.

items.example: Here to help you with client support!

image.description: Base64-encoded avatar image for the bot. This image will represent the bot in conversations and the list of bots.

channel_participation.description:
Channel participation capabilities of the bot. Defines how the bot can interact in channels.
Maximum limit: 3.
Allowed values:
  • listen_message: Bot can listen to messages in channels.
  • send_message: Bot can send messages to channels.
  • auto_follow_thread: Bot will automatically follow threads it participates in.

calls.description:
Enables or disables the Bot Calls feature for this bot. Bot Calls let the bot initiate voice alerts to targeted users for urgent, high-priority notifications. When set to enabled, the bot's Call Handler becomes active and triggers on call state events: ringing, answered, ended, declined, missed, offline, and busy.

Allowed values:
  • enabled: Bot can initiate voice calls to users. Bot calls override the recipient's Do Not Disturb setting, ensuring delivery of critical alerts.
  • disabled: Bot cannot initiate voice calls.
Notes:
  • Individual subscribers can toggle bot calls on or off from their own Bots and Tools settings.
  • Organization admins can override and lock this setting for all users via Resource Management in the Admin Panel.
  • When calls is enabled, you can optionally add a call_handler to the bot to handle call state events programmatically.

BotUpdateResponse.description: Response returned after successfully updating a bot.

url.description: API endpoint URL for the updated bot resource.

data.description: The updated bot object reflecting all applied changes.

unique_name.description: Unique identifier of the bot (cannot be changed after creation).

name.description: Updated display name of the bot.

description.example: Your CRM co-pilot providing instant access to customer data and insights within Cliq.

scope.description: Updated access level of the bot.

status_messages.description: Updated status messages for the bot.
example: Here to help you with CRM support!

calls.description: Indicates whether bot calls are enabled or disabled.

handlers.description: Handlers currently configured for the bot.

execution_type.description: Script execution engine used by the bot.

status.description: Current operational status of the bot.

type.description: Bot category. Always custom for user-created bots.

default.description: Indicates whether this is the default bot.

subscriber_count.description: Number of users subscribed to the bot.

BotEdit.description: Payload for updating an existing bot. All fields are optional.

name.description:
Updated display name of the bot. Must be unique across the platform.
Note: Updating the display name does not affect the unique_name, which is permanently assigned by Cliq and cannot be changed.
Maximum length: 20 characters.

description.example: Your CRM co-pilot providing instant access to customer data and insights within Cliq.

scope.description:
Updated access level.
Allowed values:
  • organization: Bot is available to all users in the organization.
  • team: Bot is available only to specified teams within the organization.
  • personal: Bot is available only to the creator but can be shared with other users.

team_ids.description:
List of team_ids that are allowed to access the bot when scope is set to team.
Maximum limit: 4 team IDs can be provided, and this is mandatory when the bot's scope is set to team.

status_messages.description:
Status messages shown for the bot. These messages will be displayed in the bot's profile or when users interact with the bot.
Maximum limit: 10 messages. Maximum length: Each message can be up to 50 characters long.

items.example: Here to help you with CRM support!

channel_participation.description:
Updated channel participation capabilities of the bot. Defines how the bot can interact in channels.
Maximum limit: 3.
Allowed values:
  • listen_message: Bot can listen to messages in channels.
  • send_message: Bot can send messages to channels.
  • auto_follow_thread: Bot will automatically follow threads it participates in.

execution_url.description:
The server URL where the bot's webhook events are sent. Applicable only for Webhook bots.
This field can be updated independently to redirect the bot to a new endpoint without modifying any other configuration.

calls.description:
Enables or disables the Bot Calls feature for this bot. When set to enable, the bot can initiate voice alerts to users and its Call Handler becomes active, firing on call state events: ringing, answered, ended, declined, missed, offline, and busy.

Allowed values:
  • enabled: Bot can initiate voice calls. Calls override the recipient's Do Not Disturb setting.
  • disabled: Bot cannot initiate voice calls.
Notes:
  • Individual subscribers can toggle bot calls on or off from their Bots and Tools settings.
  • Org admins can override and lock this setting for all users via the Admin Panel.

example.name: CRM Bot
example.description: Your CRM co-pilot providing instant access to customer data and insights within Cliq.
status_messages: Here to help you with CRM support!

HandlerCreate.description: Payload for creating a new handler.

type.description:
Handler type to create.
Allowed values:
  • welcome_handler: Executes when a user subscribes to the bot, providing a personalized greeting or onboarding message.
  • message_handler: Triggers on incoming messages to the bot, enabling dynamic responses based on message content.
  • mention_handler: Fires when the bot is mentioned in a conversation, allowing it to respond contextually.
  • context_handler: Executes based on specific contextual triggers defined by the bot's logic.
  • participation_handler: Activates when users interact with the bot in channels, such as joining or leaving.
  • incoming_webhook_handler: Handles events from external systems via incoming webhooks.
  • menu_handler: Defines custom menu actions for the bot, providing interactive options to users.
  • call_handler: Responds to bot call events, such as ringing, answered, ended, etc.

script.description:
Deluge script source code to execute for the handler.
Maximum size is 250KB.
Note: Applicable only for Deluge bots. Do not pass script for Webhook bots - use permissions instead.

permissions.description:
List of data attributes to forward to your server in the webhook payload when the handler fires.
Note: Applicable only for Webhook bots. Do not pass permissions for Deluge bots - use script instead.
Maximum: 5 permissions per handler.
Allowed values:
  • chat: Chat context (chat ID, type, participants).
  • message: Message content and metadata.
  • user: Sender's user profile details.
  • location: Sender's location data.
  • attachments: Attachment metadata and download info.
Not all permissions are valid for every handler type. Refer to the permissions availability table in the Create a handler endpoint description.

name.description:
Display name for the menu action, where this name will be shown as the menu action label.
Maximum length: 15 characters.
Note: Applicable only for menu_handler type.

icon.description:
• Custom icon reference for bot menu actions. This icon will be displayed alongside the menu action in the UI.
  • Refer to icons for supported menu action icons that can be used to enhance the UI of the handler's menu action.
Note: Applicable only for menu_handler type.

sub_actions.description:
List of sub-actions for menu handlers.
  • Sub-actions allow you to create nested menu options under a main menu action, providing a structured and organized way to present multiple related actions to users.
  • Maximum limit: 5 sub-actions can be defined for a menu handler.
  • Each sub-action includes a name, optional icon, and its own action definition, allowing for complex interaction flows within the bot's menu system.
Note: Applicable only for menu_handler type.

example.name: Bot Menu Action

icon.description:
Custom icon reference for the sub-action.
Refer to icons for supported icons that can be used to enhance the UI of the sub-action.

HandlerEdit.description: Payload for updating an existing handler.

script.description:
Updated Deluge script source code for the handler.
Maximum size is 250KB.
Note: Applicable only for Deluge bots.

permissions.description:
Updated list of data attributes to forward to your server in the webhook payload.
Note: Applicable only for Webhook bots.
Maximum: 5 permissions per handler.
Allowed values:
  • chat: Chat context (chat ID, type, participants).
  • message: Message content and metadata.
  • user: Sender's user profile details.
  • location: Sender's location data.
  • attachments: Attachment metadata and download info.
Not all permissions are valid for every handler type. Refer to the permissions availability table in the Create a handler endpoint description.

name.description:
Updated display name for the menu action.
Maximum length: 15 characters.
Note: Applicable only for menu_handler type.

icon.description:
Updated custom icon reference for menu handler variants.
Note: Applicable only for menu_handler type.

sub_actions.description:
Updated list of sub-actions for menu handlers.
  • Sub-actions allow you to create nested menu options under a main menu action, providing a structured and organized way to present multiple related actions to users.
  • Maximum limit: 5 sub-actions can be defined for a menu handler.
  • Each sub-action includes a name, optional icon, and its own action definition, allowing for complex interaction flows within the bot's menu system.
Note: Applicable only for menu_handler type.

example.name: IT Help Desk

BotAssociateChannelRequest.description: Payload to associate bot with a channel.

channel_unique_name.description:
Unique name of the channel to associate with the bot. This is the identifier used in API calls to specify the channel for bot association.
To retrieve the unique name of a channel, refer to CHANNEL_UNIQUE_NAME in the Glossary.

BotMessage.description: Payload for sending a message from a bot. At least one of text, card, or slides must be provided. To target specific recipients, include user_ids (comma-separated user IDs, up to 100) or set broadcast: true to reach all subscribers.

text.description:
Plain-text or rich-text message content.
Maximum length: 5000 characters.

buttons.description:
Array of action buttons attached to the message. Each button executes an action when clicked.
Maximum: 5 buttons.
For the full button object structure and supported action types, refer to the Buttons documentation.

slides.description:
Array of structured content blocks (slides) rendered below the card inside the message. Sits at the top level alongside text and card - not nested inside card. Most commonly paired with the modern-inline card theme. Maximum 20 slides. Maximum total size: 10,000 characters.

Supported slide types:
  • table - Renders a data table. Requires data.headers (array of strings) and data.rows (array of objects).
  • list - Renders a bulleted list. data is an array of strings.
  • label - Renders labelled key-value pairs. data is an array of objects with label and value.
  • images - Renders images inline. data is an array of image URL strings.

title.description: Optional heading displayed above the slide content.

data.description:
Content payload for the slide. Structure depends on type:
  • table: Object with headers (array of strings) and rows (array of objects).
  • list: Array of strings.
  • label: Array of objects, each with label and value.
  • images: Array of image URL strings.
  • text: A string containing the text content to display.

card.description: Message card object. Use to render a structured interactive card - poll, modern-inline, or prompt. For the full card object structure, available themes, and field definitions, refer to the Message Cards documentation.

bot.description: Custom sender details to override the bot's default display name and avatar for this message.

name.description: Display name shown as the message sender instead of the bot's default name.

image.description: Publicly accessible HTTPS URL of the avatar image to use for this message.

styles.description: Style settings for the message. Controls visual rendering such as background highlight colour and markdown processing.

background.description:
Background highlight colour for the message bubble.
Allowed values: red | yellow | green | blue | grey

markdown.description: Set to true to enable Markdown parsing in the text field.

user_ids.description:
Comma-separated list of user ZUIDs or emails to send the message to specific recipients.
Maximum 100 user IDs can be provided.

reply_to.description:
Message ID to reply to. The new message will be posted as a reply to the specified message.
Maximum length: 100 characters.

notify_edit.description: Set to true to send a notification to recipients if the message is edited after being sent.

message_topic.description: Topic string used as the thread title when a thread is created from this message. Maximum length: 255 characters.

mark_as_read.description: Set to true to automatically mark this message as read for the recipient.

sync_message.description: Set to true to send the message synchronously. The response will include chat_id and message_id.

thread_chat_id.description: Chat ID of an existing thread to post the message into. Maximum length: 100 characters.

thread_message_id.description: Message UID from which a thread has been originated. Used to associate the message with a specific thread.

post_in_parent.description: Set to true to also post the message in the parent chat or channel when sending to a thread.

thread_title.description:
Custom title for a newly created thread.
Maximum length: 100 characters.
